proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide and those who are not.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_abiraterone and those who are not.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_docetaxel and those who are not.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it (PARP inhibitor activity in BRCA2-enriched prostate cohort).
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 3.72 mo on enzalutamide vs 3.75 mo off. Welch t-test p=0.153.
Mean PFS 3.74 mo on abiraterone vs 3.75 mo off. Welch t-test p=0.776.
Mean PFS 3.73 mo on docetaxel vs 3.75 mo off. Welch t-test p=0.381.
Mean PFS 3.83 mo on olaparib vs 3.74 mo off. Welch t-test p=0.0033 — small but significant positive overall effect (suggests subgroup driver).
Mean PFS 3.74 mo on Lu177-PSMA vs 3.74 off. p=0.872.
Mean PFS 3.81 mo on pembrolizumab vs 3.74 off. p=0.137 — trend toward higher PFS but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (ECOG PS 1 and 2 each have lower mean PFS than ECOG 0).
Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease) have shorter pfs_months than mcrpc=0 patients.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than visceral_mets=0.
Patients with liver_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than liver_mets=0.
Patients with bone_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than bone_mets=0.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear relation).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear relation).
Higher baseline psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher gleason_score is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest ECOG1 vs ECOG0 and ECOG2 vs ECOG0
analyses.result_summary: ECOG 1 vs 0: -1.19 mo (p~0). ECOG 2 vs 0: -2.30 mo (p~0). Strong monotonic prognostic effect.
ECOG 2 vs 0 PFS difference = -2.305 mo (p~0).
mCRPC vs non-mCRPC PFS = -0.52 mo (p=1.6e-181). Strong negative prognostic effect.
Visceral mets associated with -0.048 mo lower PFS (p=0.035). Small but significant.
Liver mets PFS difference = -0.002 mo (p=0.948). No significant main effect.
Bone mets PFS difference = +0.011 mo (p=0.624). No significant main effect.
PFS rises 0.495 mo per g/dL of albumin (p=1.5e-166). Strong positive prognostic.
PFS falls -0.0004 mo per U/L of LDH (p=1.8e-04). Significant negative.
PFS falls -0.0033 mo per ng/mL PSA (p=3.5e-260). Strong negative prognostic.
PFS falls -0.0005 mo per U/L of ALP (p=0.0066). Modest negative effect.
PFS falls -0.012 mo per g/dL hemoglobin (p=0.022). Direction is opposite the hypothesized positive relation; effect is small.

PFS rises 0.174 mo per year of age (p~0). Direction is opposite the hypothesized negative relation: in this cohort older patients have longer PFS, consistent with selection toward less aggressive disease in elderly.
PFS falls -0.075 mo per % weight loss (p=9.9e-226). Strong negative prognostic.
Gleason score has no significant univariate effect on PFS (-0.006/unit, p=0.486). Becomes significant only after adjustment.
CRP has no significant univariate effect on PFS (-0.0006/unit, p=0.507).
NLR has no significant univariate effect on PFS (+0.002/unit, p=0.577).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation on pfs_months: BRCA2-mutated patients receiving olaparib have substantially longer PFS than expected from the additive effects (PARP-inhibitor synthetic lethality).
There is a positive interaction between treatment_lu177_psma and psma_high on pfs_months: PSMA-high patients receiving Lu177-PSMA have longer PFS than expected.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months: MSI-high patients receiving pembrolizumab have longer PFS than expected.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +1.619 mo (p=4.3e-61). Subgroup means: BRCA2+/olaparib+ = 5.22 mo, BRCA2+/olaparib- = 3.67, BRCA2-/olaparib+ = 3.67, BRCA2-/olaparib- = 3.74. Strongly supports synthetic-lethality predictive interaction.
Interaction coefficient = -0.017 mo (p=0.74). Subgroup PFS values are essentially identical across cells (~3.73-3.75). No predictive interaction detected.
Interaction coefficient = -0.34 mo (p=0.15). Direction opposite hypothesized; n=79 in MSI+/pembro+ cell limits power. No significant predictive effect detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ar_v7_positive is associated with shorter pfs_months (poor prognostic).

There is a negative interaction between treatment_enzalutamide and ar_v7_positive on pfs_months: AR-V7-positive patients gain less PFS from enzalutamide than AR-V7-negative patients (AR-V7 is a known mechanism of resistance to AR-targeted therapy).
There is a negative interaction between treatment_abiraterone and ar_v7_positive on pfs_months: AR-V7-positive patients gain less PFS from abiraterone.

analyses.result_summary: AR-V7+ subgroup PFS within enzalutamide-untreated stratum is 3.74, very similar to AR-V7-. No clear univariate detrimental effect.
Interaction coefficient = +0.003 mo (p=0.96). Subgroup PFS values 3.72-3.76 across all four cells. No interaction detected.
Interaction coefficient = +0.048 mo (p=0.33). No significant interaction; direction opposite hypothesized (small).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between treatment_enzalutamide and mcrpc on pfs_months: mCRPC patients respond differently to enzalutamide than non-mCRPC patients.
There is an interaction between treatment_abiraterone and mcrpc on pfs_months.
There is an interaction between treatment_docetaxel and mcrpc on pfs_months.
There is an interaction between treatment_enzalutamide and gleason_score on pfs_months.
There is an interaction between treatment_abiraterone and gleason_score on pfs_months.
There is an interaction between treatment_docetaxel and gleason_score on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction = -0.049 mo (p=0.18). Not significant.
Interaction = +0.030 mo (p=0.45). Not significant.
Interaction = +0.080 mo (p=0.042). Marginal positive interaction: docetaxel may be slightly more beneficial in mCRPC subgroup, but effect size is small and only borderline significant.
Interaction = +0.0001 mo per Gleason point (p=0.997). No effect.
Interaction = -0.031 mo per Gleason point (p=0.11). Not significant.
Interaction = +0.002 mo per Gleason point (p=0.92). No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for ECOG, albumin, LDH and treatment, higher crp_mg_l remains associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjustment, higher nlr remains associated with shorter pfs_months.
Albumin retains its strong positive prognostic effect on pfs_months after adjustment for ECOG, LDH, NLR, CRP and treatments.
Docetaxel benefit on pfs_months differs in visceral_mets vs non-visceral patients (interaction).
Docetaxel benefit on pfs_months differs in liver_mets vs non-liver patients (interaction).
Docetaxel benefit on pfs_months differs in bone_mets vs non-bone patients (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted CRP coefficient = +0.0003 mo per mg/L (p=0.71). CRP does not contribute independently after adjustment.
Adjusted NLR coefficient = +0.003 mo per unit (p=0.51). NLR does not contribute independently.
Adjusted albumin coefficient = +0.495 mo per g/dL (p=6.4e-196). Effect essentially unchanged from univariate; albumin remains a robust independent positive prognostic.
Interaction = +0.052 mo (p=0.29). Not significant.
Interaction = -0.024 mo (p=0.66). Not significant.
Interaction = +0.012 mo (p=0.81). Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with diabetes_mellitus=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without diabetes.
Patients with hypertension=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without.
Patients with copd=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without.
Patients with chronic_kidney_disease=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without.
Patients with heart_failure=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without.
Patients with coronary_artery_disease=1 have shorter pfs_months.
Patients with atrial_fibrillation=1 have shorter pfs_months.
Patients with venous_thromboembolism_history=1 have shorter pfs_months.
Patients with autoimmune_disease=1 have different pfs_months than patients without.
Patients with depression_anxiety_diagnosis=1 have shorter pfs_months.
Patients with prior_malignancy=1 have shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: DM PFS difference = -0.029 mo (p=0.19). Not significant.
HTN PFS difference = -0.024 mo (p=0.18). Not significant.
COPD PFS difference = +0.019 mo (p=0.35). Not significant; direction opposite hypothesis.
CKD PFS difference = +0.033 mo (p=0.23). Not significant; direction opposite hypothesis.
CHF PFS difference = -0.003 mo (p=0.92). Not significant.
CAD PFS difference = +0.0001 mo (p=1.0). No effect.
Afib PFS difference = -0.037 mo (p=0.28). Not significant.
VTE history PFS difference = -0.031 mo (p=0.41). Not significant.
Autoimmune PFS difference = +0.055 mo (p=0.18). Not significant.
Depression/anxiety PFS difference = -0.045 mo (p=0.061). Borderline.
Prior malignancy PFS difference = -0.043 mo (p=0.12). Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months in race_ethnicity='black' patients differs from the rest of the cohort (test for racial disparity).
Mean pfs_months in race_ethnicity='hispanic' patients differs from the rest of the cohort.
Mean pfs_months in race_ethnicity='asian' patients differs from the rest of the cohort.
Mean pfs_months in race_ethnicity='white' patients differs from the rest of the cohort.
Uninsured patients (insurance_type='uninsured') have shorter pfs_months than insured patients.
Medicaid patients have shorter pfs_months than other-insured patients.
Privately-insured patients have longer pfs_months than other-insured patients.
Medicare patients have different pfs_months than other-insured patients.
Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter pfs_months than urban patients.
Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Black vs other PFS difference = -0.004 mo (p=0.89). No disparity detected.
Hispanic vs other PFS difference = -0.008 mo (p=0.76). No disparity.
Asian vs other PFS difference = +0.057 mo (p=0.14). Trend toward higher PFS but not significant.
White vs other PFS difference = -0.008 mo (p=0.66). No disparity.
Uninsured vs other PFS difference = -0.019 mo (p=0.68). Not significant.
Medicaid vs other PFS difference = -0.004 mo (p=0.88). Not significant.
Private vs other PFS difference = -0.013 mo (p=0.48). Not significant.
Medicare vs other PFS difference = +0.018 mo (p=0.33). Not significant.
Rural vs urban PFS difference = +0.015 mo (p=0.50). No disparity detected.
PFS change per education year = -0.003 mo (p=0.40). Not significant; direction opposite hypothesized.
PFS change per pack-year = +0.0002 mo (p=0.51). Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 25 candidate SNPs (snp_*) is associated with pfs_months at a Bonferroni-corrected significance threshold of 0.05/25 = 0.002.
snp_rs4986893 (a CYP2C19 loss-of-function variant) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative direction per allele).
snp_rs429358 (APOE) shows a univariate association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: loop ols pfs_months ~ snp_x for each SNP; sort by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Best SNP p-value across 25 SNPs is 0.0145 (rs4986893). Bonferroni threshold = 0.002. No SNP survives multiple-testing correction; the genome-wide screen is null.
Per-allele effect = -0.092 mo (p=0.0145). Direction matches hypothesis but does not survive Bonferroni correction.
Per-allele effect = +0.047 mo (p=0.064). Borderline; not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After full multivariable adjustment for prognostic features and other treatments, the positive interaction of treatment_olaparib with brca2_mutation on pfs_months persists (refined predictive interaction).
After full multivariable adjustment, no positive interaction of treatment_lu177_psma with psma_high on pfs_months emerges (confirming null from iter 3).
After full multivariable adjustment, no positive interaction of treatment_pembrolizumab with msi_high on pfs_months emerges.

After full multivariable adjustment, no negative interaction of treatment_enzalutamide with ar_v7_positive on pfs_months emerges (AR-V7 does not behave as a predictive resistance biomarker in this cohort).
Gleason score becomes a significant negative prognostic factor for pfs_months after adjustment for the other prognostic features (despite null univariate effect).
ECOG, mCRPC, albumin, LDH, PSA, weight loss, visceral mets and Gleason score are each independently significant prognostic factors for pfs_months in the multivariable model (R^2 ~ 0.95).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + visceral_mets + bone_mets + liver_mets + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + hemoglobin_g_dl + crp_mg_l + nlr + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + psa_ng_ml + gleason_score + weight_loss_pct_6mo + treatment_enzalutamide + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_docetaxel + treatment_olaparib + treatment_lu177_psma + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation + brca2_mutation + treatment_lu177_psma:psma_high + psma_high + treatment_pembrolizumab:msi_high + msi_high + treatment_enzalutamide:ar_v7_positive + ar_v7_positive', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full model R^2=0.95, n=50000.
Adjusted olaparib:brca2 interaction = +1.437 mo (p<1e-300). Predictive interaction persists in full model.
Adjusted lu177_psma:psma_high interaction = +0.0005 mo (p=0.96). Null confirmed.
Adjusted pembrolizumab:msi_high interaction = +0.045 mo (p=0.39). Null confirmed; direction now slightly positive but not significant.
Adjusted enzalutamide:ar_v7_positive interaction = +0.003 mo (p=0.80). No predictive interaction.
Adjusted gleason coefficient = -0.011 mo per Gleason point (p=2.4e-08). After adjustment Gleason is a small but highly significant independent negative prognostic factor.

Adjusted ECOG -1.15 (p~0); mCRPC -0.376 (p~0); albumin +0.479 (p~0); LDH -0.0005 (p=1e-86); PSA -0.0027 (p~0); weight loss -0.076 (p~0); visceral mets -0.020 (p=6.5e-05); Gleason -0.011 (p=2e-08). All independently significant in full multivariable model.
